message:
test(core): migrate tests/benchmarks to runtime-compat + add socket throughput and recovery drill tests

Migrate all frankenterm-core tests and benchmarks from direct tokio timer
and I/O imports to the runtime_compat abstraction layer, and add two new
test modules.

Benchmark migrations:
- compression_bypass.rs: replace `tokio::time::timeout` with
  `runtime_compat::timeout` (2 call sites).
- native_events_latency.rs: replace `tokio::time::timeout` with
  `runtime_compat::timeout` (4 call sites).
- pdu_pipelining.rs: replace `tokio::time::sleep` with
  `runtime_compat::sleep` (1 call site in mock server batching delay).

New benchmark:
- socket_throughput.rs (218 LOC): Criterion benchmark exercising raw Unix
  socket throughput via the runtime_compat Unix socket layer. Measures
  single-writer streaming, concurrent reader/writer, and small-message
  ping-pong patterns at multiple buffer sizes.

Test migrations:
- asupersync_unix_socket.rs: shorten socket paths using FNV-style name
  hashing to stay within Unix socket path length limits. Add two new tests:
  `unix_socket_binary_pdu_frame_round_trip` (length-prefixed binary
  frames) and `unix_socket_connect_unreachable_fails_within_timeout`.
- distributed_security_e2e.rs: replace `tokio::io::*` with
  `asupersync::io::*`, replace `tokio::net::TcpListener/TcpStream` with
  `asupersync::net::*`, replace `tokio_rustls::TlsAcceptor/TlsConnector`
  with `asupersync::tls::*`, replace `tokio::time::timeout` with
  `runtime_compat::timeout`, update TLS constructor calls and TCP
  shutdown to match asupersync API.
- egress_tap_integration.rs: replace `tokio::time::sleep` (3 sites).
- event_annotation_tests.rs: replace `tokio::time::sleep` (2 sites).
- perf_integration_tests.rs: replace `tokio::time::sleep` (6 sites).
- runtime_compat_tests.rs: replace `tokio::time::sleep` (1 site).
- web.rs: replace `tokio::time::{sleep, timeout}` and
  `tokio::time::Instant` with runtime_compat equivalents and
  `std::time::Instant` (8 call sites).

New test module:
- recorder_recovery_drills.rs (371 LOC): End-to-end recovery drill tests
  for the flight recorder subsystem, verifying segment persistence through
  simulated crash/restart scenarios, write-ahead log replay, and partial
  flush recovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
